goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PKTN (*Oc1ccc(Oc2ccc(C(=O)c3ccc(cc3)*)cc2)cc1)
assumptions: polymer_rules.json defaults are validated for this class (confidence-gated); class confidence stays HIGH (PCFF/charges/electrostatics/size decisions D-01..D-04 unchanged). plan_mode is reasoned because the equilibration gate cannot be met by any feasible MD for this rigid-backbone aromatic polymer (D-05 carve-out), and multi-member class requires member disambiguation.

Member identified as PEEK from SMILES: repeat unit -O-C6H4-O-C6H4-C(=O)-C6H4- has exactly 2 ether oxygens + 1 ketone carbonyl (E-E-K topology). This is the defining PEEK signature; PEK is E-K (1 ether), PEKK is E-K-K (2 ketones). make_deterministic_plan.py cannot resolve member from multi-member experimental_tg_K dict and defaulted tg bracket to 433 K (PEKK). Pinned PEEK member values: Tg=418 K, amorphous density=1.263 g/cm3, K=[4.0,5.8] GPa.

Glassy-K route: Murnaghan NPT +/-1000 atm at 300 K (npt_prod300_out.data) is the primary BM method. Born+NVT removed pipeline-wide 2026-06-21: PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility gave K_Born 8-15x too high (failed on PMMA4/PVC1/PEEK1). Fallback: 3-direction uniaxial deformation if Murnaghan fit_converged=False or B0_prime outside [4,20].
Born retired: Born stage removed from pipeline per memory born_stage_retired (2026-06-21). Born REMOVED from planned_stages; murnaghan replaces it as primary mechanical stage.

uncertainties.name: rigid_backbone_chain_relaxation_incomplete
uncertainties.evidence: Terminal relaxation of the rigid PEEK aromatic backbone at dp=15/770 K is hundreds of ns (empirical PEEK1: C(t) decayed 0.1% over ~2 ns, extrapolated tau_relax ~1.9e9 ps, ~100x tau_Rouse ~2.2 ns; MSD_max 83 A^2 << Rg^2 569 A^2). Consequence: Tg will be overestimated beyond standard MD cooling-rate bias. Multirate extrapolation using the 40 K/ns anchor is the planned mitigation. literature_anchor probe (search_ff_literature) would pin amorphous-PEEK density and Tg targets to bound this bias.

uncertainties.name: amorphous_density_below_reference
uncertainties.evidence: PEEK1 measured 300 K density ~1.193 g/cm3 vs amorphous-PEEK reference 1.263 g/cm3 (~5.5% low). The commercial 1.30-1.32 figure is semicrystalline. Lower density biases bulk modulus K LOW — K result should be reported with this caveat. Recorded as mechanical-track caveat.

uncertainties.name: hardware_optimum
uncertainties.evidence: hardware_policy.values_are_benchmarked=false AND directional_probe.measured_on is 4xA800/32-core while live host is 4xRTX-6000/18-core (host mismatch). Default by_forcefield[pcff]={engine:kokkos, mpi:1, gpu_per_run:1} retained; no hardware override in decided_params. D-08 confidence: low.

decided_params.tg_window_note: Narrowed 750->250 (500 K span) to 700->350 (350 K span) following PEEK1 budget analysis: full 3-rate sweep over 500 K ~16.4 ns simulated (~28 h) with only ~3 h budget margin (too tight). 350 K window scales all 3 rates by 0.70 -> ~11.5 ns -> ~20 h, restoring real margin. Still brackets exp Tg 418 K and expected MD-Tg ~500-540 K with points each side of the knee. All 3 rates [40,160,400] retained; the 40 K/ns anchor is non-negotiable for rate->experiment extrapolation. To widen back to 750->250 requires user approval.

evidence.claim: PCFF via EMC. PEEK2020 validates COMPASS II (Class II family, same potential form as PCFF) for PEEK: Tg=424-429 K vs exp 418 K (+1-3%). PCFF covers aromatic ketone carbonyl and aryl ether torsions via Class II cross terms; Sun1994 is the FF origin.
Supporting validation: Afzal2021 PCFF amorphous PEEK thermomechanical properties.
Supporting validation: Hayashi2022 PCFF PEEK density/modulus.
Supporting validation: PEEK2020 COMPASS II Tg validation.

alternatives.rejected_because: No PEEK-specific GAFF2 Tg/density validation; GAFF2 Tg error >45% for PMMA documented; PCFF has direct Class-II analogue validation.

evidence.claim: PCFF uses bond-increment charges internally (class2 BIC scheme). PEEK has significant polar groups (ketone C=O, aryl ether O) but these are captured by PCFF BICs. No QM step required for EMC PCFF path.

Ketone carbonyl C=O and aryl ether oxygens carry significant partial charges (|q|>0.1e). PPPM required per electrostatics_decision_guide for heteroatom-containing backbones. Neglecting long-range Coulomb biases density/Tg by 5-20% for polar polymers.

alternatives.rejected_because: Polar backbone (C=O, O) with |q|>0.1e — long-range Coulomb mandatory per policy; lj_cut gives up to 20% density error.

evidence.claim: DP=32 satisfies the Fox-Flory hard floor (DP>=20 for Tg targets, per decision_policy.json:system_size.require) and DP>=30 satisfies the require_glassy carve-out for demotion of C(t)/MSD to advisory (decision_policy.json:equilibration). Both policy requirements are met at dp=32. This matches the planner's original prior (dp=32/nchain=10) from the PEEK1 approved plan.

Cell size estimate: 36 atoms/monomer (all-atom PCFF, with H) x dp=32 x nchain=10 = 11,520 atoms. Above 10k threshold; 1 GPU per run retained (benchmark support for multi-GPU absent on this host — see D-08).
evidence.method: rdkit Chem.AddHs count on stripped SMILES (*Oc1ccc(Oc2ccc(C(=O)c3ccc(cc3)*)cc2)cc1 -> 36 aa atoms/monomer)

evidence.claim: Klajmon2023 density convergence threshold ~9,000 g/mol (~20 repeat units for PEEK, MW_ru=288 g/mol); dp=32 MW ~9,216 g/mol is just at threshold — density should be well-converged.

alternatives.rejected_because: DP=15 violates the Fox-Flory hard floor (DP>=20 per decision_policy.json:system_size.require) and also prevents use of the require_glassy C(t)/MSD carve-out (requires DP>=30). A class note cannot override a fixed require clause. Rejected per critic round-1 HARD findings.
Clears Fox-Flory floor but not the require_glassy DP>=30 threshold; C(t)/MSD demotion would still be a policy violation at DP=20.

decisions.choice: ACCEPT require_glassy carve-out (DP>=30 satisfied): static-structure + density gated; C(t)/MSD demoted to advisory non-blocking

evidence.claim: The require_glassy carve-out (decision_policy.json:equilibration) is now legally satisfied: DP=32>=30 AND regime=glassy (PEEK Tg=418 K >> 300 K equil-check temperature). Under this carve-out, C(t)/MSD can be demoted to advisory (non-blocking) without violating the fixed framework.

Physical basis: terminal relaxation of the rigid PEEK aromatic backbone at melt is hundreds of ns — outside feasible MD. PEEK1 empirical: melt-NVT (770 K, 1 ns production) C(t) decayed 0.1% over ~2 ns (gate 10%), extrapolated tau_relax ~1.9e9 ps (~100x tau_Rouse). MSD_max 83 A^2 << Rg^2 569 A^2. Standard EXTEND (1-2 ns) cannot move C(t). The require_glassy carve-out exists precisely for this class of polymer.
Static structure ALL PASS (PEEK1 reference: Rg CV 20%<30%, P2 0.038<0.10, density-homogeneity CV 15%<25%) and 300 K NPT density converged. These remain the gating criteria.

Tg-overestimate caveat: incomplete chain relaxation freezes configuration and biases apparent Tg HIGH beyond standard MD cooling-rate bias. Multirate rate->experiment extrapolation keeping the 40 K/ns anchor is the planned mitigation.
Density caveat: amorphous-cell reference is 1.263 g/cm3 (quenched amorphous PEEK). PEEK1 measured 1.193 g/cm3 (~5.5% low vs amorphous reference). Lower density biases bulk modulus K LOW.

alternatives.choice: FAIL the run (enforce overall_pass=True strictly, no carve-out)
alternatives.rejected_because: require_glassy carve-out is legally available at DP=32>=30; static structure and density gate are clean; strict failure wastes a physically sound cell.

alternatives.choice: Extend melt NVT by 1-2 ns (standard EXTEND remedy)
alternatives.rejected_because: 0.1% C(t) decay over 2 ns (PEEK1 empirical) means 1-2 ns extension cannot move C(t). MSD/density are fixed-volume-NVT-invariant. Extension burns budget with no convergence gain.

decisions.choice: bilinear fit R^2>=0.8; sweep 700->350 K brackets exp 418 K and expected MD-Tg 500-540 K

evidence.claim: T sweep 700->350 K (350 K span) brackets experimental Tg=418 K with 282 K headroom above and 68 K below (the glassy regime). Expected MD-Tg ~500-540 K is also bracketed. With tg_t_step_K=20, n_bins=17 — adequate for bilinear fit. Multirate [40,160,400] K/ns provides rate->experiment extrapolation to correct the overestimate bias.

The 40 K/ns rate is the accuracy anchor for the rate->experiment log-linear extrapolation. It MUST NOT be dropped even if budget is tight; narrowing the temperature window is the correct lever (see tg_window_note).

decisions.choice: Murnaghan NPT +/-1000 atm at 300 K (glassy primary); 3-direction deform fallback

evidence.claim: PEEK is firmly glassy at 300 K: experimental Tg=418 K >> 300 K. MD Tg expected ~500-540 K. Glassy BM via Murnaghan NPT +/-1000 atm compression using npt_prod300_out.data (Wu 2019 Comput. Mater. Sci. validated; symmetric, cell-size independent).
Born+NVT REMOVED pipeline-wide 2026-06-21. PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility: K_Born 8-15x too high, Var(P) ~10^7x too high, failed on PMMA4/PVC1/PEEK1. Even a 4-ns run (PEEK1) gave K_T=-49.6 GPa.
Fallback: 3-direction uniaxial deformation if Murnaghan fit_converged=False or B0_prime outside [4,20]. isotropy_delta_pct>=20% = hard FAIL.
K will be biased LOW due to amorphous-cell density ~5.5% below reference (1.263 g/cm3). Report K with caveat; do not treat a low K vs exp [4.0, 5.8] GPa as a method failure.

alternatives.rejected_because: PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility confirmed 2026-06-21 across 3 runs; K_Born 8-15x too high. Permanently retired.

decisions.choice: engine=kokkos, gpu_per_run=1, mpi=1 (by_forcefield[pcff] default; cell ~11,520 atoms > 10k, no multi-GPU benchmark on this host)

evidence.claim: FF=PCFF, by_forcefield[pcff]={engine:kokkos, mpi:1, gpu_per_run:1}. Cell size ~11,520 atoms (36 all-atom/monomer x dp=32 x nchain=10) — above 10k threshold. Benchmark cell 3,020 atoms; 11,520 is ~3.8x (outside [0.5x,2x] clean range for directional_probe adoption). Policy allows gpu_per_run>=2 above 10k only with benchmark support; none exists on this host (host mismatch: probe on 4xA800 vs live 4xRTX-6000). Retain by_forcefield[pcff] default: engine=kokkos, mpi=1, gpu_per_run=1. No hardware override in decided_params.

values_are_benchmarked=false AND directional_probe.measured_on is 4xA800 40GB/32-core while live host is 4xRTX-6000 24GB/18-core (host mismatch). Adopt default by_forcefield[pcff]; no hardware override in decided_params. Benchmark evidence (42.8 ns/day kokkos vs 5.8 ns/day GPU-pkg, 2026-06-20, contended) is a strong signal but was measured on a different host class. Reference ns/day not baked in as a claim.
PCFF+PPPM: mpi=1 is CANONICAL with KOKKOS (kspace runs on GPU, not CPU-serial; old gpu-package mpi>=4 rule does NOT apply). Single-rank KOKKOS: skip mpirun, use env CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=N lmp.

alternatives.rejected_because: KOKKOS is ~7.9x faster than GPU-pkg (3,020-atom contended benchmark); fallback available if KOKKOS binary unavailable.

advisory_non_blocking.rationale: REQUIRE_GLASSY CARVE-OUT (decision_policy.json:equilibration): C(t) decay and MSD>Rg^2 are demoted to ADVISORY (non-blocking). Carve-out is legal: DP=32>=30 AND regime=glassy (PEEK Tg=418 K >> 300 K). Physical basis: terminal relaxation of PEEK aromatic backbone at melt is hundreds of ns (PEEK1: C(t) decayed 0.1% over ~2 ns, tau_relax ~1.9e9 ps); 1-2 ns EXTEND provably cannot move C(t). Static-structure homogeneity + density convergence are the gating criteria. Backbone_types must be passed explicitly to equil-check (avoid auto-detect failure on aromatics).

success_criteria.note: EXTEND accepted as terminal for this run: static structure passes and further NVT cannot move C(t) (see equil stage advisory). Do NOT loop extensions chasing C(t) — budget better spent on Tg sweep. backbone_types must be passed explicitly (aromatic auto-detect unreliable).

planned_stages.scope_note: Budget-safe 700->350 K window (350 K span). Full class default 750->250 K (500 K) was ~28 h leaving only ~3 h margin vs 48 h budget; 350 K window is ~20 h. All 3 rates [40,160,400] K/ns kept. The 40 K/ns anchor is NON-NEGOTIABLE for rate->experiment extrapolation. To widen back to 750->250 K requires user approval.

planned_stages.caveat: K will be biased LOW: amorphous-cell density ~1.193 g/cm3 is ~5.5% below amorphous-PEEK reference 1.263 g/cm3 (lower density -> lower stiffness). Report K with this caveat; do not treat a low K vs exp [4.0, 5.8] GPa as method failure. Ref: polymer_rules.json:classes.PKTN.exp_density_note.

findings: APPROVED round 2. Gate 1a: PKTN confidence=high; reasoned mode is the over-checking direction (corrective multi-member PEEK disambiguation + D-05 rigid-backbone carve-out), not a confidence-gate bypass — full step-3 enforcement applied. Round-1 HARD findings resolved: D-04 dp=32 clears Fox-Flory (>=20) AND require_glassy (>=30); D-05 require_glassy legally satisfied (DP=32>=30 AND glassy, Tg=418>>300). D-01/D-03/D-07 (evidence_required) each cite DOI/citation + non-empty alternatives + full criteria. D-08 hardware unpinned (no engine/gpu_per_run/mpi in decided_params -> policy-derived kokkos/mpi=1/1GPU default; mpi=1 canonical on KOKKOS, not the GPU-package anti-pattern). All planned_stages have valid tracks + success_criteria; dominant uncertainty named with valid literature_anchor probe.
round_1_findings_addressed: FINDING 1 (D-05): RESOLVED. require_glassy carve-out now legally satisfied: DP=32>=30 AND regime=glassy. D-05 choice, evidence, and equil-stage advisory_non_blocking rationale updated to cite decision_policy.json:equilibration:require_glassy explicitly.

FINDING 2 (D-04): RESOLVED. dp_typical raised to 32, nchain=10. D-04 choice, evidence, and alternatives updated: DP=32 satisfies Fox-Flory hard floor (DP>=20) and require_glassy (DP>=30). Cell size updated to 11,520 atoms; D-08 evidence updated accordingly.
D-08 cell size updated from ~4,320 to ~11,520 atoms (>10k); by_forcefield[pcff] default retained (no benchmark for multi-GPU on this host).

provenance.based_on: PEEK1 approved reasoned plan (2026-06-18, 2 critic rounds) + born_stage_retired (2026-06-21)
